body.title: Audit skills: structural directives vs behavioral questions
body.result: Finding types section added to audit-design + audit-ticket skills; levers doc updated
body.intention: I intend that UU audit skills distinguish structural anti-patterns (directives) from behavioral complexity (questions), so audits are accurate about what an AI can and cannot safely assert.
body.description:
Currently audit-design and audit-ticket issue directives on both structural and behavioral findings. Adopted from +ddai design-reviewer (2026-06-04): structural anti-patterns can be asserted confidently; behavioral complexity should be questioned, not directed — the AI cannot safely judge business logic or non-obvious design choices.

Update both skill files to categorize findings as:
- STRUCTURAL (directive: must change) — abstraction leaks, missing log calls, wrong inheritance, scope boundary violations, rule violations
- BEHAVIORAL (question: why is this...) — complex logic, unusual patterns, non-obvious design choices, unexplained depth

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/audit-design/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/skills/audit-ticket/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** none apply (skill file changes only)
**Scope boundary:** Only audit-design and audit-ticket. No other audit skills changed here.
**Completion criteria:** Both skill files have a Finding types section with STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIORAL distinction. The categorization is applied in the audit output format. Verified by running audit-design on a sample ticket and observing two-category output.
